source_text:
Health Department Launches Vaccination, Screening Drive

Chennai, July 3: The state health department has launched a special vaccination and health screening drive for children and senior citizens across government hospitals and primary health centres.

Officials said the campaign will focus on routine childhood vaccination, diabetes screening, blood pressure checks and basic health counselling for elderly residents. Mobile medical teams will also visit selected rural and semi-urban areas where access to hospitals is limited.

Health workers have been asked to identify children who missed scheduled vaccines and encourage parents to complete the pending doses. Senior citizens will be screened for common lifestyle-related conditions and referred to higher medical centres if needed.

The department said the drive is preventive in nature and is not linked to any disease outbreak. Officials added that early detection of health risks can reduce complications and hospitalisation.

Doctors said many families postpone regular health checks unless symptoms become serious. They urged people to use the campaign as an opportunity for basic screening and medical advice.

The drive will continue for two weeks, and local health officials have been asked to submit daily reports on attendance, referrals and medicine availability.
